games.explanation: Edward Cabrera spent the year being a perfectly serviceable righty against right-handed bats and a walk-prone mess against lefties, and the Giants happen to roll out one of the best lefty bats in baseball hitting third. Devers has chewed up right-handed pitching all year to the tune of a .391 xwOBA, Arraez doesn't strike out against anyone, and Cabrera's walk rate against southpaws sits north of 13%, which is how short outings start. Ray isn't pristine, but he's the steadier arm in a park playing small, with the wind knocking down the only thing Cabrera does well. Getting plus money on the better lineup in a game the board has priced as a coin flip is the entire point.

Yesavage's overall line looks like a future ace until you separate it by handedness, where the rookie has been a completely different pitcher against righties than lefties. Baltimore happens to roll out a righty-heavy top of the order with Henderson, Alonso, and Ward, and against same-side bats Yesavage is coughing up a .332 xwOBA with barrels and hard contact in every direction. Brandon Young is no prize, but the Jays are a small enough favorite that Toronto's edge is supposed to come from their starter, and that edge thins out considerably once you read the splits. Getting a plus-money price on the better Elo team in a coin-flip game is the entire argument, and +130 is generous for it.

The market is pricing Rasmussen like a frontline arm and Miami like a tomato can, when the gap between these teams is much smaller than the number suggests. Rasmussen has been quietly hittable to right-handed bats this year, which is exactly the look Stowers and Lopez want, and Kyle Stowers in particular has been demolishing righties at a .395 xwOBA clip that does not care about reputation. Gusto is no prize, but the Rays lineup beyond Caminero and Aranda thins out fast, and a closed roof neutralizes the one park edge Tampa usually leans on. Getting plus money on the better Elo team in a projected one-run game is the entire argument, and +118 is generous enough to make it.
